status_notes: Initial sweep entry — not deeply researched.
official_name: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada
notes: Federal privacy regulator, enforces PIPEDA. See also registry/entity/ca/gc/priv.json for the regulator itself.

match_nodes.description: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

data.official_name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
data.description: Canada's baseline federal private-sector data-protection law, in force since 2001. Governs collection, use, and disclosure of personal information in the course of commercial activity.
data.notes: The federal government has made multiple attempts to replace PIPEDA (CPPA under Bill C-27, died on the order paper Jan 2025; now Bill C-36 / PPCDA as of June 2026) — see secid:regulation/priv.gc.ca/ppcda-bill-c36. PIPEDA remains in force until a successor passes.

match_nodes.description: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act (Bill C-36) — proposed PIPEDA successor

data.official_name: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act
data.description: Third federal attempt at private-sector privacy reform (after the CPPA in the prior Bill C-27, which died on the order paper January 2025). First reading June 15, 2026. Would replace PIPEDA, introduce a new enforcement body (the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada), and penalties up to CAD 25M or 5% of global revenue.
data.notes: Status: proposed, pre-second-reading as of this sweep entry (2026). Not yet in force — track alongside PIPEDA (secid:regulation/priv.gc.ca/pipeda) which remains current law. Revisit as the bill progresses.
